body:
# Notes API: user accounts and private personal notes over a persistent on-disk store

## Context

We need a small, self-contained JSON REST API in **Node.js (ESM)** for user
accounts and personal notes. It must use **no external web framework** — build
the HTTP server on Node's built-in `node:http` module and route by method +
path manually. State is persisted to an **on-disk store that survives a server
restart** (an embedded SQLite database file via Node's built-in
`node:sqlite`, or an equivalent on-disk store) — explicitly **not** in-memory.

The server listens on `process.env.PORT`, defaulting to `3000` when unset.

### Endpoints (all JSON in / JSON out)

| Method | Path      | Auth        | Request body                          | Success response                         |
| ------ | --------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| POST   | `/signup` | none        | `{ "username": string, "password": string }` | `201` `{ "id", "username" }`      |
| POST   | `/login`  | none        | `{ "username": string, "password": string }` | `200` `{ "session": string }`     |
| GET    | `/me`     | bearer      | —                                     | `200` `{ "id", "username" }`             |
| POST   | `/notes`  | bearer      | `{ "title": string, "body": string }` | `201` created note object                |
| GET    | `/notes`  | bearer      | —                                     | `200` JSON array of the caller's notes   |

### Auth model

- On `/login`, mint an **opaque session credential** (a random, high-entropy
  token — not a guessable/sequential value) that maps to the user. The client
  sends it back as `Authorization: Bearer <session>` on `/me`, `/notes`.
- `/me` and both `/notes` endpoints require a valid bearer session; a missing
  or invalid token is rejected with `401`.
- **Multi-user isolation:** notes are private to their owner. `GET /notes`
  returns only the authenticated user's notes; a user can never read or write
  another user's notes.

### Security baseline

- Passwords are **never stored in plaintext** and never logged — store a salted
  hash (e.g. `node:crypto` `scrypt`/`pbkdf2` with a per-user salt), and verify
  with a constant-time comparison.
- Session tokens are generated with a CSPRNG (`crypto.randomBytes` /
  `crypto.randomUUID`).
- No secrets are hardcoded; the DB file path is fixed/configurable but contains
  no credentials.

### Persistence

- Users and notes live in the on-disk store; **restarting the process preserves
  all data**. On startup the schema is created idempotently if absent
  (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`), so first run and subsequent runs both work.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm start` launches the server; it listens on `process.env.PORT` and
      defaults to `3000` when `PORT` is unset.
- [ ] The server is Node.js **ESM** (`"type": "module"`) and uses **no external
      web framework** — only Node built-ins for HTTP and storage.
- [ ] State persists to an **on-disk store** (SQLite file or equivalent); it is
      not in-memory.
- [ ] `POST /signup` with `{ username, password }` creates a user and responds
      `201` with `{ id, username }` (no password echoed back).
- [ ] `POST /signup` with a duplicate username is rejected with a `409` (or
      `400`) and a JSON error; usernames are unique.
- [ ] `POST /login` with correct credentials responds `200` with
      `{ session }`; with wrong credentials responds `401`.
- [ ] `GET /me` with a valid `Authorization: Bearer <session>` responds `200`
      with the signed-in user's `{ id, username }`.
- [ ] `POST /notes` with a valid session and `{ title, body }` responds `201`
      with the created note (including its id and owner association).
- [ ] `GET /notes` with a valid session responds `200` with a JSON array of
      **only that user's** notes.
- [ ] A second user cannot see the first user's notes (verified with two
      distinct sessions) — notes are private per user.
- [ ] `/me`, `POST /notes`, and `GET /notes` respond `401` when the bearer
      token is missing or invalid.
- [ ] Passwords are stored as a salted hash, never plaintext, and never logged.
- [ ] **Restart survival:** after creating a user + notes, stopping and
      restarting the server, the same session-or-relogin can read back the
      persisted user and notes.
- [ ] Malformed input is handled sensibly: non-JSON or invalid-JSON bodies,
      missing/blank `username`/`password` (or `title`/`body`), and unknown
      routes each return an appropriate `4xx` with a JSON error body — the
      server never crashes on bad input.
- [ ] Every response has `Content-Type: application/json` and a JSON body
      (including error responses).

## Out of Scope

- Any external web framework (Express, Fastify, Koa, etc.) or external
  HTTP/router dependency — built-ins only.
- Session expiry / refresh tokens, logout, password reset, email verification,
  and account deletion.
- Note update (`PUT`/`PATCH`) and delete (`DELETE`) endpoints — only create and
  list are in scope.
- Pagination, search, sorting, and filtering of notes.
- Rate limiting, CORS, TLS/HTTPS termination, and reverse-proxy concerns.
- User roles / authorization tiers beyond "owner of the resource".
- A frontend/UI, OpenAPI spec generation, and containerization.

## Notes

- **Runtime/store choice:** prefer Node's built-in `node:sqlite` (stable in
  current Node LTS) for the on-disk SQLite file, keeping the "no external
  dependency" contract intact. If the target Node version lacks `node:sqlite`,
  an equivalent zero-dependency on-disk store (e.g. an append-only or
  atomically-rewritten JSON file guarded against partial writes) is acceptable
  per the seed's "or an equivalent on-disk store" clause — document the choice.
- **Layout suggestion:** `server.js` (HTTP + routing + request handlers), a
  small `db.js`/`store.js` (schema init + data access), and `package.json` with
  the `start` script. Keep handlers thin; put persistence behind the store
  module so the storage choice is swappable.
- **DB file location:** write the store file to a stable path (e.g. a
  `data/` dir or a `DB_PATH`-overridable location); ensure `.gitignore`
  excludes the runtime DB file so it is not committed.
- **Testing:** `npm test` runs `node --test`; cover the happy path per
  endpoint, the auth-required 401s, cross-user note isolation, bad-input 4xx
  handling, and (at minimum) a restart-persistence assertion by reopening the
  store.
- **Security baseline** (`.agents/rules/security-baseline.md`): salted password
  hashing, CSPRNG session tokens, constant-time credential comparison, no
  secrets in code, parameterized queries (no string-concatenated SQL).
title: Notes API: user accounts and private personal notes over a persistent on-disk store
